summary: M5-TASK-GRAPH-001 is CLOSED after PR #108 merged into origin/main at 8b771272a9cfa790282043fcd092c1ce59fd0117, local main synchronized, and both PR Gate checks passed. The accepted M5 task graph remains planning-only; downstream M5 implementation tasks are dependency-ready but not started by this closure.

PR #108 merged; origin/main advanced to 8b771272a9cfa790282043fcd092c1ce59fd0117 and local main fast-forwarded.
HEAD, origin/main, and main all resolved to 8b771272a9cfa790282043fcd092c1ce59fd0117 before closure branch edits.
M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 is the next dependency-ready task.
Closed qa_reviewer thread after completed ACCEPT.
Agent was not found by the platform at closure time; no close result is fabricated. The systems REVIEW handoff remains validated and recorded.

decisions: Close M5-TASK-GRAPH-001 only after PR #108 entered main and local main matched origin/main.
Keep downstream M5 tasks in their planning-created DRAFT state; taskctl ready selects M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 as the next machine action.

Preserve all M5 Human Gates: formal M5 passage, manual node battle, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial decisions, paid services/accounts/secrets, frozen architecture/platform/core changes, branding, cultural-risk decisions, and irreversible migrations.
Do not summarize M4_GATE as systems acceptance; the PASS_WITH_LIMITS process limit remains unresolved but accepted.
risks: M5 formal passage remains a Human Gate and is not passed by this task graph closure.
The replacement systems_architect thread was not close_agent-addressable at closure time; the repository records the valid handoff and the failed close attempt explicitly.
requested_action: Merge this closure checkpoint, sync main, then start M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 with gameplay_designer writer and systems_architect/qa review as required by its task file.
